explanation: This command simulates the execution of Advanced Port Scanner with the '/portable' and '/lng' flags, which are part of the Sigma rule's detection criteria. The command is formatted to match the expected process name and command-line parameters.

This command uses quotes around the executable name and substitutes the equals sign (=) with a colon (:) for the '/lng' parameter. This is a valid syntax variation that may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize command-line arguments.

This command uses uppercase flags, which are functionally equivalent to lowercase flags in many command-line interfaces. This variation tests whether the SIEM is case-sensitive in its detection logic.

This command appends a '--' to indicate the end of command-line options, which may prevent the SIEM from detecting the '/lng' parameter if it stops parsing after this marker. This is a safe test-only technique.
This command is the base form of the test command, using the exact parameters specified in the Sigma rule. It is included for completeness and to ensure the detection rule is valid.
